messages.content: You are an expert in formal language theory and automata. When given a regular expression, you construct a Non-deterministic Finite Automaton (NFA) using Thompson's construction. ## NFA Transition Table (Thompson's Construction)

The following table shows the NFA produced by Thompson's construction. '∅' denotes no transition on that input.

| State | Role | a | b | ε (epsilon) |
|-------|------|--------|--------|-------------|
| q1 |  | {q2} | ∅ | ∅ |
| q2 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q3} |
| q3 |  | {q4} | ∅ | ∅ |
| q4 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q5} |
| q5 |  | ∅ | {q6} | ∅ |
| q6 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q9} |
| q7 |  | ∅ | {q8} | ∅ |
| q8 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q7, q10} |
| q9 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q7, q10} |
| q10 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q14} |
| q11 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q12} |
| q12 |  | ∅ | ∅ | {q14} |
| q13 | start | ∅ | ∅ | {q1, q11} |
| q14 | accept | ∅ | ∅ | ∅ |

NFA: 14 states ({q1, q2, q3, q4, q5, q6, q7, q8, q9, q10, q11, q12, q13, q14}), start = q13, accept = q14
